name: Human
description: Standard human forces. Most common faction in Beamstrike. Can use all standard equipment, weapons and armour types.

name: Hibevor
description: The most technologically advanced alien race. Physically slim, 5–6ft tall. Long-range snipers who avoid close combat. Use jet-packs, personal shields, and their signature Power Sphere Projector.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. Use jet-packs.
special: Hibevor firer: +1 to hit
Count Hibevor PA armour as AD class for damage purposes
Damage modifier +1 when target is Hibevor or Grey
Hibevor may not choose hand-to-hand combat
Before advancing, Hibevor squad must pass 1d6 roll of 2 or less (or if leader present, move as player wishes)
Opportunity to take cover: 1d6 roll of 3 or less required (or if troops are in PA or vehicle)
Use jet-packs and personal shields

name: Growwlan
description: Proud warrior clan aliens, 7.5ft tall. Value personal bravery and single combat. Settle disputes with laser duels. Ride the fearsome 'Wrok' Warbird mounts. Excel at night fighting.
special: Growwlan units must pass troop roll NOT to advance toward enemy if not in view of a unit chief (commander lowers required score by 1)
Growwlan units must pass troop roll to hide behind cover if not in view of a unit chief
Growwlan usually only use lasers for duelling — may not be armed with any other laser weapon except a laser pistol
Growwlan may not target leaders or medics except in close or hand-to-hand combat
Growwlan see any aide to ranged combat as dishonourable — do not use targetters
Growwlan night vision: +1 to hit when firing at night or in dark
All Growwlan firearms: +1 to damage roll
Growwlan do not use jet-packs
Sniper troop skill may NOT be used

name: Thuntra
description: Huge 12ft reptilian ex-slaves of the Ferrapur. Single-minded and fearsome in hand-to-hand. Hard to kill (–1 damage modifier against them). Sworn enemies of the Ferrapur.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. Slow but unstoppable.
special: Thuntra must lose three-quarters of their number before becoming shaken
Thuntra may not have higher than 'regular' troop class
Thuntra are never affected by 'Glancing Hit' results — treat as 'no effect'
Fearsome H2H: humans wishing to engage Thuntra must first succeed a troop roll or choose a different close combat option
Easy to hit (+2 to hit number), but hard to kill (–1 to damage table roll)
Thuntra do not use jet-packs
Thuntra may only fight alongside Ferrapurs in battles set prior to the end of the Ten Thousand Years War
Thuntra use the Mecha special movement types STOMP and OVERTURN; classed as hull class 3

name: Centaling
description: 7–8ft tall four-legged humanoid mercenaries. Superb scouts and sappers. Temperamental loners — difficult to lead, brutal to enemies. In H2H, get two throws of the dice.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. Very fast.
special: Firer targeting a Centaling: +1 to hit
Centaling H2H: two throws of the dice — once with all bonuses (hand-held melee weapons), once at +0 (forelegs)
Centalings do not use jet-packs
Centalings fight independently — no benefits from leaders of other races. Once rallied, fight independently again
No more than 10% of any army may be Centaling
Each individual has Scout and Sapper skills
To kill a Centaling requires 2 'kill' damage hits (internal organs duplicated)

name: Ferrapur
description: 8ft cat-like bipeds. Stealth specialists with chameleon armour. Developed advanced power-lasers. Snipers are feared throughout the Four Galaxies. Excel at close combat.
special: May only use advanced weapons and the Ferrapur sniper cannon
All Ferrapur have scout ability (affects concealment range etc.)
Ferrapur do not use jet-packs
Stealth cloaks often used
Damage modifier: +1 when firing Advanced Laser Pistol/Rifle
Ferrapur may only fight alongside Thuntras in battles set prior to the end of the Ten Thousand Years War

name: Replican
description: Advanced androids and artificial intelligences. No morale or suppression. Can field Mimick infiltrators that disguise as enemy troops. Replican vehicles do not have crew.
special: Morale and Suppression rules are NOT applicable to Replican armies
Replican forces do not suffer Night or Bad weather firing penalties
Replicans in squads must stay within 2 inches of at least one other squad member (not the standard 3 inches)
Replican vehicles are computer-controlled — no crew

name: SPUG
description: Insectoid bipeds, 6ft tall. Slow-moving and poor eyesight in dark/smoke. Hive structure: packs of 4–6 cells. Use shard weapons and toxcannons. Spugnaughts are feared exo-suits.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. Slow. Spugnaught: 10/10/8/6/4.
special: –1 additional to hit penalty when firing through smoke, bad weather or night time (does NOT apply to Spugnaught or Heavy Infantry)
Spugs may use advanced weapons and sniper rifles along with Spug-specific types listed
Spugs are not subject to morale rules but follow normal cohesion and suppression rules
Spugs may use jet-packs (Assault Jump Spugs are a common troop type)
Spugs may not fight alongside any other race — universally despised
Pack organisation: 4–6 Cells per Hive. Cell = 2–3 packs of 6 troopers. Spug unit without leader in cohesion suffers –2 firing penalty

name: Grey
description: Ancient 4ft aliens who 'seeded' Earth. Highly intelligent, abhor direct conflict. Use advanced grav technology, Q-travel pods, invisibility cloaks and P-beam weapons. Shields common.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. Grav vehicles and Q-travel pods.
special: Greys may only use the weapons/devices described in the supplement
Greys cut their losses and beam out sooner than most — shaken when morale points are at 2/3 instead of 1/2
Greys will not fight their own kind
Greys will not fight other aliens if games are set during the Great Ten Thousand Years War
Greys are physically slender and weak: +1 damage roll penalty on hits against them
Grey Combat Construct squad: must stay within 3 inches of control scout — if control scout killed, squad suppressed and attempts to move back to nearest Grey mothership

name: Bug
description: Chitinous semi-intelligent insectoid aliens. No manufactured weapons — claws and natural weapons only. Hive mind, multiple castes: Runner, Hunter, Terror, Flyer, Cerebral, Queen, Colossus.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. No penalties for night/bad weather. Cerebral burrows at 6"/move.
special: No penalties for movement or ranged combat in night or bad weather
Runner and Terror: fanatic troops, Glancing Hit results ignored
Bug armies will not fight other bug armies (will merge — unless between queens)
Bugs CANNOT use manufactured weapons, jet packs or other equipment — claws and natural weapons only
Runner/Terror: firer gets –1 to hit
Hunter/Flyer: firer gets –2 to hit
Cerebral/Colossus: firer gets +2 to hit
Hunter/Terror claws: +3 H2H (power sword equivalent)
Colossus claws: +5 H2H (power axe equivalent)
Cerebral tunnelling: may surface anywhere along its burrow within 1 inch of exit hole each move phase

name: K'Kree
description: Centaur-like herbivores from the Two Thousand Worlds (T'tkhahk Xeng Kirr). Militaristic vegetarians who fight in large herds. Xenophobic towards carnivores. Conservative and claustrophobic.
movementNotes: See alien figure movement chart. Quadruped: excellent open-ground speed. K'Kree Fast Infantry: 10/8/7/6/5.
special: All military K'Kree encountered will be at least Veteran troops
K'Kree cannot use other races' weaponry due to odd hand structure — may use any Human AND Alien chart weapons
K'Kree squads are herds of 10–20 warriors and NEVER leave a man behind
K'Kree +3 H2H bonus when fighting carnivores (Ferrapur, Thuntra, Bugs, etc.)
K'Kree will not seek any enclosed cover (no bunkers or enclosed buildings)
K'Kree will not transport in small enclosed vehicles
Coherency distance: 2 inches (not the standard 3)
K'Kree H2H: two throws — once with all bonuses (hand-held melee weapons), once at +0 (forelegs)
No K'Kree troop types are independent — all will be in herds

name: Kra'vak
description: Non-canonical race — not in official alien supplement. Alien race with battle rage ability. Uses gauss weaponry.
special: Normal: +1 H2H
Enraged: +2 H2H, -1 to damage roll when target, firer gets -1 to hit them
Battle rage: +3 H2H, -2 to damage roll when target

name: Orcoid
description: Non-canonical race — not in official alien supplement. Alien race. Good close combat and sturdy.
special: Damage modifier vs Thuntra/Orcoid: -1
